283.reason: [The condition `curPos+1 == lineLenth` is incorrect. After the while loop, `curPos` points to the last character before `*/`, so `curPos+1` equals `lineLenth` only if the `*/` is at the end of the line. However, the logic should check if the `*/` was not found in the current line, meaning the comment spans multiple lines. The correct condition should be `curPos+1 >= lineLenth` or simply `else` after the single-line `*/` check fails.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition `curPos+1 == lineLenth` is incorrect. After the while loop, `curPos` points to the last character before '*', so `curPos+1` equals `lineLenth` only if the last character is '*', but the loop stops when `curPos+1 >= lineLenth` or the next char is '*'. This condition should be `curPos+1 >= lineLenth` to correctly detect when the end of the line is reached without finding "*/".]
